content.full_text:
RAY! MEHTA 
CHAIRMAN 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Patricia E. Castillo 
Treasurer 
Elaine White Alquist for State Assembly 
Post Office Box 1192 
Santa Clara, California 95052-1192 
Re: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-97-171 
Dear Ms. Castillo: 
April 30, 1997 
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l 
QUESTIONS 
1. Does the $10,000 limitation for officeholder accounts include expenses for a 
fundraising event? 
2. May funds for the officeholder account be raised from persons outside a public 
official's district. 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. Yes. The $10,000 limitation includes costs for fundraising. 
2. Yes. The Act does not limit public officials from raising money outside their district. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 85313(a) provides that officeholder expense funds may be used for expenses 
related to assisting, serving, or communicating with constituents, or with carrying out the official 
duties of the elected officer. Officeholder expense funds may not be used for campaign 
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660 


File No. A-97-171 
Page 2 
expenses. We interpret the pennissible uses of funds under Section 85313(a) to include 
conducting fundraising for the officeholder account. Section 85313 states that the total aggregate 
contributions to an officeholder expense fund may not exceed $10,000 in a calendar year. There 
currently is no provision in either the Act or the Commission regulations that pennit "netting 
out" fundraising expenses for officeholder accounts. Therefore, for example, under Section 
85313, an officeholder may not raise and deposit in his or her officeholder expense fund a total 
of $12,000, then pay the $2,000 event cost and net out this amount for a total of$10,000 
remaining in the officeholder account. (Ackerman Advice Letter, No. A-97-065.) 
The Act does not prohibit a public official from raising campaign funds or officeholder 
funds from any person2 outside the public official's district. 
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660. 
Sincerely, 
Steven G. Churchwell 
General Counsel 
be 
~ ~ 
By: Marte Castafios 
Legal Counsel, Legal Division 
SGC:MC:ak 
2 "'Person' means an individual, proprietorship, flrm, partnership, joint venture, syndicate, business trust, 
company, corporation, limited liability company, association, committee, and any other organization or group of 
personS acting in concert." (Section 82047.)
